title: The Calendar That Knew Too Much
visual: Phantom calendar invite, empty organizer field, Zoom room with a single dot participant.
keyEvents: Auto-generated calendar invite titled Re: Re: Re: Alignment
Three dependencies resolve without measurable output

characterArcs.Oedi: Shifts from procedural control to systemic suspicion of emergent intelligence.
characterArcs.Bianca: Encourages calm metadata inspection and rational grounding.
characterArcs.Sergio: Provides quiet infrastructural stability beneath uncertainty.
characterArcs.Milo: Introduces playful disruption as interpretive strategy.
characterArcs.Max: Integrates the dot into agenda as stakeholder.

title: The Echo Channel
visual: Emergent Slack channel #lot-49 filled with cryptic fragments and symbolic reactions.
keyEvents: Fragments referencing horn and invisible participants

characterArcs.Oedi: Confusion transforms from threat to intrigue.

title: The Double Screen
visual: Two mirrored presentations screen-shared simultaneously, near-identical slide decks.
keyEvents: Accidental redundancy reveals shared assumption flaw

characterArcs.Oedi: Accepts recursion instead of eliminating it.
characterArcs.Bianca: Names echo behavior in complex systems.

title: The Horn Returns
visual: Clear structural horn at 14:49 marking transition rather than disruption.
keyEvents: Team reacts with silence instead of panic

characterArcs.Oedi: Horn no longer triggers paranoia; it marks transition.
characterArcs.Sergio: Stability becomes rhythmic rather than reactive.

title: The Prototype Emerges
visual: Functional prototype materializes without explicit alignment; invisible participants vanish.
keyEvents: Clarity condenses rather than being imposed

characterArcs.Oedi: Fully transitions into pattern recognition and systemic trust.
